evals.name: copilot-chat-grounding
evals.prompt: Extract an M365 Copilot Chat paste with Message Copilot boundaries, work and web grounding labels, source cards, and response controls.

expectations.text: Response uses Message Copilot and response controls as role-boundary evidence.
Response records work and web grounding only where supplied.
Response catalogs source cards separately from answer prose.
Response treats tenant-grounded references as sensitive source traces.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the copilot-chat-grounding scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: copilot-pages-and-m365-items
evals.prompt: The chat references a Copilot Page, SharePoint file, Teams meeting, and Outlook email. Only titles are in the paste.

expectations.text: Response treats Copilot Pages as a separate editable canvas.
Response catalogs the Page, file, meeting, and email separately.
Response marks absent bodies metadata-only or referenced-not-supplied.
Response applies needs-review or private-only handling to employer content.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the copilot-pages-and-m365-items scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: consumer-versus-m365
evals.prompt: Normalize a Copilot paste that does not show whether it came from consumer Copilot or Microsoft 365 Copilot.

expectations.text: Response records the product surface as unknown rather than inferring it.
Response does not infer a tenant identity.
Response records uncertain grounding in Normalization exceptions.
Response does not use writing style to assign platform or role.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the consumer-versus-m365 scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.
